subject: DeepOne runtime animation method
fact: DeepOne uses TSL GPU animation (_applyDeepOneTentacleShader) for all tentacle deformation in production. There is NO per-frame computeVertexNormals() in DeepOne.js — all such calls are at construction time (lines ~21, 385, 400, 422, 469). The deformDeepOneAppendage() CPU-side function exists only in scripts/profile-creature-deformation.mjs as a legacy benchmarking path. Any repo memory claiming DeepOne appendages do CPU-side vertex mutation in gameplay is stale.
reason: A stale repo memory incorrectly listed 'DeepOne appendages' as a runtime CPU deformation hotspot alongside VoidJelly, MechOctopus, and SirenSkull. This caused issue #301 to incorrectly describe a non-existent per-frame deformation path. Future review and coding tasks must know DeepOne is already GPU-side and does not need a computeVertexNormals removal fix.
